question: What is the Dungeon Train?
narration: Carriage {carriage}. That is where the dark reached you. Do you know it now? The Dungeon Train does not stop — it only goes on, deeper, forever.

question: What is the Dungeon Train?
narration: {carriage} carriages into the dark. Few ride so far before the line takes them. Fewer still board again.

question: What is the Dungeon Train?
narration: Carriage {carriage}. Barely aboard, and already the dark had you. The line is patient — it can afford to be.

question: Why are you here?
narration:
You felled {mobs} things in the dark, and bled for it with {hearts} hearts.
Of the {met} souls you met, {slain} you ended — and {friends} you called friend.

question: What are you meant to do?
narration: Iron and edge, gathered from the dark and carried this far. None of it was enough — perhaps that was never the point.

question: What are you meant to do?
narration: You read {books} of the line's books and still carried steel. Knowledge and a blade, and the dark took both.

question: Were they worth it?
narration: You made {friends} friends on the line, and still it took you. Perhaps that is the only number that was ever yours to keep.

question: How much of the dark did you answer in kind?
narration: {mobs} things fell to your hand before you fell to theirs. The line does not judge. It only counts.

question: These are all good questions
narration: These are not the marks of a single life. Across all {deaths} — every dark you have boarded, every time the line took you and still you returned — this is the whole of your journey.

question: These are all good questions
narration: These are not the marks of a single life. {deaths} times the dark has taken you, and {deaths} times you boarded again.

question: Will you board again, or step off into the quiet?
narration: The platform is empty. The whistle sounds far down the line. It runs without end — and so, it seems, do you.
epitaph: the {deaths}th to fall. perhaps not the last.

question: Need you even ask?
narration: The platform knows your face by now. The whistle sounds the way it always does. You already know what comes next.
epitaph: the {deaths}th to fall — and not, it seems, the last.
